question_title: What will be the value of "ICE BofA CCC & Lower US High Yield Index Effective Yield" on 2026-03-24?
summary:
The ICE BofA CCC & Lower US High Yield Index Effective Yield has been rising sharply since late February 2026, moving from 12.42% to 13.61% in ~15 trading days. This is driven by escalating Middle East tensions (BofA cross-market risk at 0.79, junk bond volatility tripled since January), surging Treasury yields (10Y from 3.95% to 4.3%), and broad credit spread widening. 

For the 7 trading days to March 24, I ran a scenario-weighted Monte Carlo simulation (500k paths): 45% continued stress (drift +0.065/day), 20% escalation with fat tails (+0.10/day), 20% stabilization (+0.02/day), and 15% de-escalation (-0.04/day). The median outcome is ~13.94%, reflecting momentum persistence over this short horizon. The distribution is slightly right-skewed due to tail risk from escalation scenarios.

factors.description: Strong recent uptrend: CCC yields rose from 12.42 to 13.61 (+1.19) in ~15 trading days, with daily drift of ~0.07
Middle East tensions escalating: BofA cross-market risk at 0.79, junk bond vol index tripled since Jan, broad market stress pushing credit spreads wider
Treasury yields surged (3.95% to 4.3% in 2 weeks), raising baseline borrowing costs that CCC yields sit atop
Possible de-escalation: Geopolitical tensions can reverse quickly if ceasefire talks progress, pulling yields back toward 13.0-13.5
CCC yields at 13.61% are elevated but not at crisis levels (2020 hit ~20%). Room exists for further rise but also gravitational pull.
Short horizon (7 trading days): momentum tends to persist, mean reversion unlikely in this timeframe. Autocorrelation measured at 0.163.

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

| Percentile | Value (%) |
|-----------|----------|
| P1 | 12.83 |
| P5 | 13.15 |
| P10 | 13.33 |
| P25 | 13.63 |
| P50 (median) | **13.94** |
| P75 | 14.24 |
| P90 | 14.53 |
| P95 | 14.73 |
| P99 | 15.21 |

**Key drivers:** The yield has been on a strong uptrend driven by Middle East tensions and rising Treasury yields. Starting from 13.61% on March 13, the median forecast for March 24 is ~13.94%, reflecting continued momentum over the short 7-trading-day horizon. The 80% confidence interval (P10-P90) spans 13.33%-14.53%, capturing both the de-escalation downside and the escalation upside scenarios.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "ICE BofA CCC & Lower US High Yield Index Effective Yield" on 2026-03-24?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42695)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series BAMLH0A3HYCEY once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=BAMLH0A3HYCEY`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-24 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
